hyperedges.label: Workspace Audit Revalidation Skill Catalog Sync

nodes.label: Bootstrap Sequence
nodes.rationale: Agent execution starts from a fixed sequence of rule, persona, checklist, agentic, memory, and scope documents so work is deterministic and auditable.

nodes.label: Repository Hard Constraints
nodes.rationale: Root instructions remain thin, official stage docs are protected by default, and secrets must be handled through Docker Secrets or mounted secret files.

nodes.label: Repository Verification Policy
nodes.rationale: Infrastructure, governance, root, and documentation changes must be verified with the smallest relevant repository checks before completion.

nodes.label: RTK Command Prefix Rule
nodes.rationale: Shell commands are intended to be prefixed with rtk to route them through a token-optimized CLI proxy.

nodes.label: MADR-Extended ADR Framework
nodes.rationale: Architecture decisions are captured with tradeoff analysis, quality attributes, validation criteria, and change history so future agents can understand and challenge the decision.

nodes.label: DORA Metrics Targets
nodes.rationale: Pipeline choices are evaluated through measurable deployment frequency, lead time, change failure rate, and recovery time targets.

nodes.label: Four Code Review Dimensions
nodes.rationale: Security, architecture, performance, and style provide a consistent review taxonomy with conflict resolution between domains.

nodes.label: STRIDE/DREAD Container Threat Model
nodes.rationale: Container trust boundaries are analyzed with STRIDE threats, DREAD scoring, and Docker-specific countermeasures before production exposure.

nodes.label: Zero-Downtime Compose Deployment Patterns
nodes.rationale: Rolling, blue-green, and canary patterns are adapted to Docker Compose and Traefik to avoid requiring Kubernetes or cloud-native infrastructure.

nodes.label: Timeline-First RCA
nodes.rationale: Incident handling emphasizes detection, timeline reconstruction, mitigation, root-cause analysis, recovery, and postmortem follow-up.

nodes.label: Runtime boundary for local agent catalog
nodes.norm_label: runtime boundary for local agent catalog

nodes.norm_label: human approved dag and n8n activation

nodes.label: Fail-closed Auth Model
nodes.rationale: Authentication failures should fail closed, with degraded mode handled only through policy and runbook procedures.

nodes.label: Non-intrusive Management Layer
nodes.rationale: The laboratory tier is designed as a management and observability layer that does not directly affect core traffic when it fails.

nodes.label: Security-boundary-first Management UI
nodes.rationale: Laboratory UIs are high-privilege management surfaces, so the architecture puts gateway chain, SSO, and allowlist controls before productivity concerns.

nodes.label: Tooling Catalog Expansion Policy
nodes.rationale: Tooling hardening keeps present runtime contracts stable while catalog items describe staged improvements such as drift detection, signing, quality gates, and distributed test topology.

nodes.label: Prometheus Grafana Loki Tempo Alloy Stack
nodes.norm_label: prometheus grafana loki tempo alloy stack

nodes.label: MinIO Object Storage for Loki and Tempo
nodes.norm_label: minio object storage for loki and tempo

nodes.norm_label: n8n queue mode and secret guard

nodes.label: Docs Taxonomy and AI Agent-first Contract Migration Specification
nodes.norm_label: docs taxonomy and ai agent-first contract migration specification

nodes.label: Agent Governance Thin Root Shim Contract
nodes.norm_label: agent governance thin root shim contract

nodes.label: infra_net Config Contract
nodes.rationale: All included Compose services must attach to the global infra_net network so service communication uses one standardized boundary.

nodes.label: infra_net IPAM Subnet 172.19.0.0/16
nodes.rationale: The subnet and gateway are fixed through the root Docker Compose IPAM configuration to make address assignment deterministic.

nodes.label: Authoritative Static IP Assignment Map
nodes.rationale: The specification preserves existing fixed IP allocations for service groups while moving them under infra_net.

nodes.label: k3d-hyhome Preservation Contract
nodes.rationale: Services that already declare k3d-hyhome must keep that network instead of replacing it during infra_net standardization.

nodes.label: Docker DNS Service Resolution
nodes.rationale: The spec excludes direct /etc/hosts edits and recommends Docker DNS as the service discovery mechanism.

nodes.label: docker compose config Verification
nodes.rationale: Merged Compose output is the verification source for subnet, network preservation, and final service attachment.

nodes.label: Execution Plans Workspace
nodes.rationale: Plans hold execution order, risk, and verification criteria before implementation begins.

nodes.label: Execution Tasks Evidence Workspace
nodes.rationale: Tasks preserve implementation work, evidence, verification results, and completion status.

nodes.label: Cross-Layer Traceability
nodes.rationale: Execution artifacts are linked back to requirements, architecture, specs, and operations documents.

nodes.label: Gateway PRD ARD ADR Chain
nodes.rationale: The gateway plan creates PRD, ARD, and ADR documents to make Traefik/Nginx architecture traceable without changing gateway implementation.

nodes.label: Identity and Access Documentation Chain
nodes.rationale: The auth plan sequences investigation, PRD, ARD, ADR, spec, README refactoring, and task evidence for identity and access infrastructure.

nodes.label: Vault Root of Trust Secret Safety
nodes.rationale: The security plan flags Vault as root of trust and requires avoiding unseal key or root token exposure during documentation.
nodes.norm_label: vault root of trust secret safety

nodes.label: Multimodel Persistence Reverse Engineering
nodes.rationale: The data plan reverse engineers existing multimodel persistence infrastructure into PRD, ARD, ADR, Spec, and task documents.

nodes.label: Kafka RabbitMQ Documentation Chain
nodes.rationale: The messaging plan builds standard docs for Kafka and RabbitMQ so service-level README information is elevated into governed docs.

nodes.label: LGTM and Alloy Documentation Alignment
nodes.rationale: The observability plan integrates fragmented LGTM stack and Alloy collector details into cross-layer documentation.

nodes.label: Thin Root Architecture
nodes.rationale: The standardization plans align documents to a thin root model where detailed policy and stage artifacts live in governed subdirectories.

nodes.label: Golden 5 Taxonomy
nodes.rationale: The workflow plan names the Golden 5 document family as the target governance taxonomy.

nodes.label: Ollama Open WebUI Local AI
nodes.rationale: The AI plan records local AI infrastructure around Ollama and Open WebUI as a governed documentation surface.

nodes.label: IaC Quality and Performance Tooling
nodes.rationale: The tooling plan covers Terrakube, SonarQube, performance testing tools, operational guides, policies, and runbooks.

nodes.label: Golden 5 Infrastructure README
nodes.rationale: The communication plan refactors the infra README around Overview, Architecture, Integration, Operations, and Governance.

nodes.label: Homer Dozzle Portainer RedisInsight Laboratory Stack
nodes.rationale: The laboratory plan consolidates fragmented service information for Homer, Dozzle, Portainer, and RedisInsight.
nodes.norm_label: homer dozzle portainer redisinsight laboratory stack

nodes.label: Open WebUI RAG Ollama Qdrant Integration
nodes.rationale: Open WebUI is planned as the AI tier user and automated RAG interface integrated with local Ollama and Qdrant.
nodes.norm_label: open webui rag ollama qdrant integration

nodes.label: GPU OOM Mitigation
nodes.rationale: The Open WebUI plan mitigates GPU OOM risk by limiting qwen3-embedding concurrency or falling back to CPU.

nodes.label: SSO and Healthcheck Separation
nodes.rationale: The plan keeps an internal healthcheck route available as a non-SSO path while user access depends on SSO.

nodes.label: Priority Score Model
nodes.rationale: The plan fixes prioritization as risk reduction, availability impact, security impact, and inverse execution effort.

nodes.label: Tier Weight Model
nodes.rationale: Tier A, B, and C group infrastructure tiers by operational criticality for optimization scheduling.

nodes.label: Quick Wins Baseline
nodes.rationale: Quick Wins lock 30-day backlog items for restart, healthcheck, no-new-privileges, resource limits, secrets, Airbyte gap closure, and traceability.

nodes.label: Common Optimizations Coverage Snapshot
nodes.rationale: The plan records common-optimizations.yml coverage as 39 of 39 service directories and 43 of 43 Compose files.

nodes.label: 2026 Q2 Q3 Quarterly Roadmap
nodes.rationale: The roadmap carries Compose policy gates, recovery rehearsals, SLO/alert alignment, performance optimization, CI policy checks, and security hardening.

nodes.label: Restart Policy Control
nodes.rationale: Tier A and B long-running services must standardize restart policy coverage.

nodes.label: Healthcheck Control
nodes.rationale: Healthcheck gaps are prioritized for operating-critical Tier A and B services.

nodes.label: No New Privileges Control
nodes.rationale: The plan introduces no-new-privileges as a default hardening principle with explicit exceptions.

nodes.label: Resource Limits Control
nodes.rationale: Tier-level minimum CPU and memory limits are documented as capacity controls.

nodes.label: Secrets and Vault Control
nodes.rationale: Sensitive value injection is standardized toward Docker secrets and Vault paths.

nodes.label: Execution Operations Traceability Cleanup
nodes.rationale: The quick wins plan includes explicit cleanup for 04.execution plans to 05.operations links.

nodes.label: Traefik Standard Middleware Chain
nodes.rationale: Gateway-owned routers receive rate-limit, retry, circuit-breaker, and gateway-standard-chain middleware.

nodes.label: Nginx Readonly Failover Model
nodes.rationale: Nginx is hardened with readonly templates, required tmpfs paths, timeout and failover behavior, and /ping healthcheck.

nodes.label: Gateway Hardening CI Gate
nodes.rationale: check-gateway-hardening.sh and ci-quality.yml enforce gateway hardening as a strict CI gate.

nodes.label: OAuth2 Proxy Secret Entrypoint Contract
nodes.rationale: OAuth2 Proxy secrets are exported from mounted secret files through a hardened entrypoint and non-root image.

nodes.label: Auth Fail-Closed Security Posture
nodes.rationale: The auth plan fixes a fail-closed posture while documenting controlled degraded-mode procedures.

nodes.label: Degraded Mode Operations
nodes.rationale: Auth outage handling is documented through policy and procedure while preserving restricted fail-closed behavior.

nodes.label: Vault Agent Health Template Contract
nodes.rationale: Vault Agent healthcheck, /vault/out volume, capabilities, template paths, and keys are stabilized before HA expansion.

nodes.label: Auto-Unseal and Remote Audit Policy
nodes.rationale: Immediate KMS/HSM auto-unseal and remote audit sink implementation are deferred into policy and transition procedures.

nodes.label: Auth Hardening Regression Repair
nodes.rationale: The security plan explicitly includes repair of the auth hardening verification script regression.

nodes.label: Data Compose Consistency Contract
nodes.rationale: The data plan fixes healthcheck, secret path, expose token, and tier label consistency before broader runtime changes.

nodes.label: Supabase Valkey SeaweedFS ksql Targets
nodes.rationale: The immediate data hardening target set is Supabase, Valkey Cluster, SeaweedFS, and ksql.

nodes.label: Data Hardening CI Gate
nodes.rationale: check-data-hardening.sh and ci-quality.yml enforce the data hardening baseline.

nodes.label: Messaging Gateway SSO Boundary
nodes.rationale: Kafka UI and RabbitMQ management paths are aligned to gateway standard chain and SSO policy.

nodes.label: Kafka RabbitMQ Management Hardening
nodes.rationale: The messaging plan fixes Kafka UI image tags, dev compose path consistency, and RabbitMQ management middleware.

nodes.label: Messaging Hardening CI Gate
nodes.rationale: check-messaging-hardening.sh and the messaging-hardening CI job guard Kafka and RabbitMQ changes.

nodes.label: Observability Gateway SSO Boundary
nodes.rationale: Observability public routes are aligned to the gateway standard chain and SSO policy.

nodes.label: Observability Health-Gated Startup Contract
nodes.rationale: The plan strengthens dependency contracts and cAdvisor healthcheck behavior to improve startup stability.

nodes.label: Loki Tempo Custom Image Hardening
nodes.rationale: Loki and Tempo Dockerfiles and entrypoints are hardened as custom runtime images.

nodes.label: Observability Hardening CI Gate
nodes.rationale: check-observability-hardening.sh and the observability-hardening job enforce the observability baseline.

nodes.label: Workflow Gateway SSO Alignment
nodes.rationale: Airflow and n8n routes are aligned with gateway standards and SSO middleware.

nodes.label: n8n Custom Image Secret Guard
nodes.rationale: The n8n custom image and entrypoint carry non-root and secret guard contracts into Compose defaults.

nodes.label: Airflow n8n Health Dependency Contract
nodes.rationale: Airflow and n8n startup behavior is strengthened with service_healthy dependencies, healthchecks, and task-runner coverage.

nodes.label: Workflow Hardening CI Gate
nodes.rationale: check-workflow-hardening.sh and the workflow CI gate prevent regressions in workflow hardening.

nodes.label: Airbyte Gap Backlog
nodes.rationale: Airbyte is intentionally kept as a backlog item because immediate deployment is a non-goal.

nodes.label: Ollama GPU Concurrency Queue Limits
nodes.rationale: The AI plan sets Ollama concurrency, queue, and resource ceilings to protect GPU capacity.

nodes.label: Open WebUI Stateful Template
nodes.rationale: Open WebUI is aligned to a stateful operations template and guarded against stateful template drift.

nodes.label: Ollama Exporter Health Gate
nodes.rationale: The Ollama exporter receives health-gated startup and healthcheck behavior.

nodes.label: AI Hardening CI Gate
nodes.rationale: check-ai-hardening.sh and the AI CI gate block hardening regressions.

nodes.label: Model Access and Log Policy Backlog
nodes.rationale: Model promotion, access separation, and log retention or masking are made executable as future policy tasks.
nodes.norm_label: model access and log policy backlog

nodes.label: Tooling Gateway SSO Boundary
nodes.rationale: SonarQube, Terrakube, and Syncthing public paths are aligned to gateway standards and SSO policy.

nodes.label: Tooling infra_net External Contract
nodes.rationale: Tooling Compose files standardize the infra_net external network declaration.

nodes.label: Locust k6 Runtime Contract
nodes.rationale: The tooling plan aligns locust-worker healthcheck behavior and k6 volume references.

nodes.label: Tooling Hardening CI Gate
nodes.rationale: check-tooling-hardening.sh and the tooling CI gate validate public path, network, and runtime contracts.

nodes.label: Tooling Catalog Expansion Backlog
nodes.rationale: Catalog expansion items are decomposed into document and task-backed work instead of immediate runtime implementation.

nodes.label: Laboratory Hardening Script and CI Gate
nodes.norm_label: laboratory hardening script and ci gate

nodes.label: Docker Compose to k3s/k3d Migration Strategy
nodes.norm_label: docker compose to k3s/k3d migration strategy

nodes.label: Docs Taxonomy and AI Agent-first Contract Migration Plan
nodes.norm_label: docs taxonomy and ai agent-first contract migration plan

nodes.label: Data Tier Persistence Availability Security Requirements
nodes.rationale: The 04-data tier exists to preserve persistence, availability, backup coverage, and security controls across all data services.
nodes.norm_label: data tier persistence availability security requirements

nodes.label: Data Tier Traceability and Stage Links
nodes.rationale: Every operations policy is expected to trace to architecture requirements and corresponding procedures.
nodes.norm_label: data tier traceability and stage links

nodes.label: Data Tier Initial Triage
nodes.rationale: The common emergency entry point starts with service status, recent logs, and disk capacity checks.

nodes.label: Vector Database Support
nodes.rationale: The guide identifies Qdrant as the high-performance vector database in the specialized analytical data tier.

nodes.label: OLAP Engine Support
nodes.rationale: The guide identifies StarRocks as the OLAP database for real-time analytics.

nodes.label: Analytics Resource and Retention Governance
nodes.rationale: Analytics operations coordinate engine resource limits, data retention policies, security controls, and audit procedures.

nodes.label: InfluxDB Time-Series Retention Policy
nodes.rationale: InfluxDB buckets must have retention limits, valid API-token writes, and separate handling for high-frequency data.

nodes.label: InfluxDB Disk Threshold Recovery
nodes.rationale: The runbook triggers recovery when health checks fail, authorization errors appear, or disk usage crosses the 80 percent threshold.

nodes.label: ksqlDB Stream Lifecycle Governance
nodes.rationale: ksqlDB stream and table creation is governed by offset-reset defaults, join review, EMIT CHANGES usage, and JVM memory limits.

nodes.label: ksqlDB Kafka and Schema Registry Dependency
nodes.norm_label: ksqldb kafka and schema registry dependency

nodes.label: ksqlDB Query Restart Recovery
nodes.rationale: The recovery path diagnoses Kafka connectivity, query state, consumer lag, and restarts failed queries or the server when needed.

nodes.label: OpenSearch Shard Replica Security Controls
nodes.rationale: OpenSearch requires replicas, JVM heap limits, HTTPS external access, IP whitelisting, and avoidance of hardcoded admin credentials.

nodes.label: OpenSearch Cluster Index Recovery
nodes.rationale: The runbook handles Red or Yellow cluster health, unassigned shards, indexing backpressure, node restarts, and shard relocation.

nodes.label: StarRocks Partition Bucket Resource Strategy
nodes.rationale: StarRocks tables require explicit partitioning, bucketing, BE resource limits, and query guardrails to avoid uncontrolled OLAP scans.

nodes.label: StarRocks Stream Load Idempotency
nodes.rationale: Stream Load jobs use labels so repeated ingestion attempts remain idempotent.

nodes.label: StarRocks FE BE Recovery
nodes.rationale: StarRocks recovery checks FE and BE health, storage paths, load-job state, and metadata restoration paths.

nodes.label: Cache and KV Persistence Eviction Standards
nodes.rationale: Cache and KV operations define persistence, memory management, access control, eviction, and recovery boundaries for Valkey and Redis-compatible stores.
nodes.norm_label: cache and kv persistence eviction standards

nodes.label: Valkey AOF LRU Secret Controls
nodes.rationale: Valkey requires AOF persistence, allkeys-lru eviction, Docker Secrets authentication, and no unauthenticated external exposure.

nodes.label: Valkey Slot Failover Recovery
nodes.rationale: The Valkey runbook restores cluster_state ok, repairs missing hash slots, and verifies connected nodes after failover or partition events.

nodes.label: Lake and Object S3 Filer Storage Boundary
nodes.rationale: Lake and object storage separates operating policies, usage guides, and recovery procedures for S3-compatible and filer-based storage.
nodes.norm_label: lake and object s3 filer storage boundary

nodes.label: MinIO Path-Style S3 Integration
nodes.rationale: Applications must use S3 path-style access and separate app credentials rather than deployment root credentials.

nodes.label: MinIO Storage Exhaustion Recovery
nodes.rationale: MinIO recovery diagnoses disk capacity, bucket usage, cluster health, and service logs before clearing data, resetting credentials, or restarting nodes.

nodes.label: SeaweedFS Master Volume Filer S3 Mount Services
nodes.norm_label: seaweedfs master volume filer s3 mount services

nodes.label: SeaweedFS Metadata and Volume Backup
nodes.rationale: SeaweedFS operations protect filer metadata, require production replication, export volumes, snapshot metadata, and enforce access-log retention.

nodes.label: NoSQL SLO Backup Scaling Governance
nodes.rationale: NoSQL operations align SLOs, backup retention, scaling guidance, and patch procedures for Cassandra, CouchDB, and MongoDB.

nodes.label: Cassandra Query-Driven Modeling
nodes.rationale: Cassandra usage emphasizes query-driven modeling, keyspace and primary-key design, SAI indexing, and avoidance of heavy ALLOW FILTERING scans.

nodes.label: Cassandra Snapshot Restore
nodes.rationale: Cassandra recovery restores corrupted data by stopping the node, preserving broken data, restoring a snapshot, fixing ownership, and restarting.

nodes.label: Cassandra Compaction Disk Risk
nodes.rationale: Compaction can temporarily double disk consumption, so Cassandra operations require free-space headroom and disk alerts.

nodes.label: CouchDB Multi-Master Replication
nodes.rationale: CouchDB usage centers on HTTP APIs, multi-master replication, checkpoints, revision-based conflict handling, views, and Mango queries.

nodes.label: CouchDB Compaction and Revision Control
nodes.rationale: CouchDB must manage append-only fragmentation, auto-compaction, revision sprawl, and shared secret consistency.

nodes.label: CouchDB Cluster Resync Recovery
nodes.rationale: The CouchDB runbook rejoins out-of-sync nodes, performs manual compaction during high fragmentation, and verifies scheduler and document revisions.

nodes.label: MongoDB Replica Set Quorum
nodes.rationale: MongoDB operations protect replica set quorum, failover behavior, arbiter availability, and Primary/Secondary replication lag.

nodes.label: MongoDB Oplog Consistent Backup
nodes.rationale: MongoDB backups use oplog-aware dumps and monitor oplog window size so secondaries can re-sync safely.

nodes.label: MongoDB Primary Failover Recovery
nodes.rationale: The MongoDB runbook restores quorum, recovers missing primaries, and performs initial sync when a secondary falls outside the oplog window.

nodes.label: Management Database Platform Metadata Store
nodes.rationale: mng-db stores platform metadata for identity, automation, workflow, n8n, keycloak, airflow, PostgreSQL, and Valkey services.

nodes.label: Management Database Secrets and Audit Controls
nodes.rationale: Management database credentials must be injected through /run/secrets, rotated periodically, and protected with DDL audit logging.
nodes.norm_label: management database secrets and audit controls

nodes.label: Management Database Bootstrap Recovery
nodes.rationale: The mng-db procedure starts services, reruns mng-pg-init for missing users or schemas, and watches mng-pg and mng-valkey logs.

nodes.label: Supabase Auth JWT Storage Realtime Controls
nodes.rationale: Supabase operations cover PostgreSQL persistence, authentication, JWT security, asset storage, websocket realtime, and edge function limits.
nodes.norm_label: supabase auth jwt storage realtime controls

nodes.label: Supabase Database Recovery
nodes.rationale: The Supabase runbook stops the stack, restores the last healthy SQL dump, restarts services, and handles initial password reset tasks.

nodes.label: PostgreSQL HA Architecture
nodes.rationale: The relational tier uses Patroni, etcd, and HAProxy to provide high availability, leader election, and routed database access.

nodes.label: Relational etcd Quorum Switchover Recovery
nodes.rationale: Relational recovery covers leader identification, etcd health, disk space, node restart, quorum recovery, switchover, and pg-router verification.

nodes.label: PostgreSQL Patroni etcd HAProxy Policy
nodes.rationale: postgresql-cluster policy requires etcd quorum, Patroni leadership management, HAProxy routing integrity, and no direct writes to individual pg nodes.

nodes.label: PostgreSQL WAL Backup Recovery Validation
nodes.rationale: The cluster requires daily full backups, real-time WAL archiving, replication-lag monitoring, and monthly restore simulation.

nodes.label: Specialized Vector and Graph Database Boundary
nodes.rationale: Specialized data operations group engines such as Neo4j and Qdrant while keeping policy, guide, and runbook concerns separated.
nodes.norm_label: specialized vector and graph database boundary

nodes.label: Neo4j Graph Database Operations
nodes.rationale: Neo4j operations govern graph database memory, page cache, TLS/Bolt access, secret-backed authentication, and daily offline dumps.

nodes.label: Neo4j Offline Dump Restore
nodes.rationale: Neo4j Community Edition backup and restore require stopping the instance, running neo4j-admin dump or load, and restarting the service.

nodes.label: Qdrant Vector Search Operations
nodes.rationale: Qdrant operations govern vector collection management, similarity search, snapshot retention, indexing optimization, API keys, TLS exposure, and unprivileged images.

nodes.label: Qdrant Snapshot Recovery
nodes.rationale: Qdrant recovery creates and restores collection snapshots, checks readyz and collections, and falls back to host-level data backups if restore fails.

nodes.label: Shared Secret Handling Guardrail
nodes.rationale: Multiple data-service guides explicitly prohibit exposing secret values and prefer Docker Secrets, secret files, API keys, or approved credential injection.

nodes.label: Shared Observability Evidence Capture
nodes.rationale: Recovery sections repeatedly require command output, health status, logs, metrics, and verification evidence to be captured after operations.

nodes.label: Shared Backup and Recovery Pattern
nodes.rationale: The 04-data guides converge on backup-first recovery, restore validation, rollback preservation, and incident evidence capture across engines.

nodes.norm_label: ollama model pull and test flow

nodes.norm_label: dlq reprocessing and quorum queue expansion

nodes.norm_label: infra_net ip mapping validation and update procedure

nodes.norm_label: lsm trees vs write ahead logging

nodes.norm_label: cs ce se self learning roadmap v1

nodes.norm_label: vector space modeling and similarity search

nodes.label: Standard ForwardAuth Pattern
nodes.rationale: New internal services are expected to integrate through Traefik labels and the shared OAuth2 Proxy ForwardAuth middleware so SSO behavior is centralized.

nodes.label: Auth Secret Rotation Synchronization
nodes.rationale: Cookie secret and client secret rotations must be synchronized across Keycloak and OAuth2 Proxy because mismatched values invalidate sessions or OIDC client authentication.

nodes.label: Keycloak Healthcheck Strategy
nodes.rationale: The management port readiness endpoint is checked through a shell TCP probe so dependent services can wait until Keycloak is actually ready during bootstrap.

nodes.label: Vault Unseal Protocol
nodes.rationale: Vault operations require checking sealed state first because sealed Vault breaks secret retrieval and automation must not attempt secret reads before unseal.

nodes.label: Polyglot Persistence Layer
nodes.rationale: The data tier separates transactional SQL, cache, vector, graph, object, search, and analytics stores so each workload uses a fit-for-purpose persistence engine.

nodes.label: Analytics Docker Secrets Policy
nodes.rationale: Analytics service passwords and tokens are governed through Docker Secrets rather than direct file edits to preserve secret hygiene.

nodes.norm_label: opensearch security basic internal auth domain
